person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Maud de Prendergast, Lady of Offaly (17 March 1242- before 1273), was a Norman-Irish noblewoman, the first wife of Maurice FitzGerald, 3rd Lord of Offaly, Justiciar of Ireland, and the mother of his two daughters, Juliana FitzGerald and Amabel. Maud was a descendant of Strongbow, the Irish kings of Leinster and Thomond, and Brian Boru. Her own descendants included most of the medieval noble families of England. She married three times; Maurice FitzGerald, 3rd Lord of Offaly was her third husband.

«b»Family«/b»
Maud was born in Ireland on 17 March 1242, the daughter of Sir Gerald de Prendergast of Beauvoir (died 1251), and his second wife, who was one of the daughters of Richard Mor de Burgh and Egidia de Lacy.

Maud had an elder half-sister, Marie de Prendergast from her father's first marriage to Maud Walter. Marie was the wife of Sir John de Cogan by whom she had issue. Maud's paternal grandparents were Philip de Prendergast, Lord of Enniscorthy, Constable of Leinster, and Maud de Quincy, a granddaughter of Strongbow, through the latter's illegitimate daughter Basilie de Clare who married Robert de Quincy, Constable of Leinster. Her great-grandfather, Maurice de Prendergast, Lord Prendergast had played a prominent part in the Cambro-Norman invasion of Ireland led by Strongbow, and was rewarded with much land in counties Wexford, Waterford, Tipperary, Mayo, Wicklow, and Cork. Her maternal great-grandparents were William de Burgh and Mór O'Brien; Walter de Lacy, Lord of Trim and Margaret de Braose.

«b»Marriages and issue«/b»
When she was a young child, Maud was married to, firstly David FitzMaurice, who died by 17 March 1249, which was her seventh birthday; her second husband was Maurice de Rochford. Between 1258 and 28 October 1259, following Maurice de Rochford's death which occurred sometime before May 1258, she married her third and last husband, Maurice FitzGerald, 3rd Lord of Offaly, Justiciar of Ireland (1238-1286). He was the son of Maurice FitzGerald, 2nd Lord of Offaly and Juliana.
Together Maurice and Maud had two daughters:

1.) Juliana FitzGerald (c.1263 Dublin, Ireland-24 September 1300), married firstly Thomas de Clare, Lord of Thomond, by whom she had four children; she married secondly Nicholas Avenel; she married thirdly Adam de Cretynges.

2.) Amabel FitzGerald, married, but was childless.

Maud died on an unknown date. In 1273, her husband Maurice married his second wife, Emmeline Longespee (1252-1291) but fathered no children by her.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Maud or Matilda de Prendergast於1242年3月17日出生在愛爾蘭庫迪 懷可福德恩尼斯可爾時，她的父親Sir Gerald de Prendergast Lord of Enniscorthy56歲，母親Matilda de Burgh14歲。她於大約1258年與Maurice FitzMaurice 3rd Lord of Offaly結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。她去世於1276年7月26日在英國北愛爾蘭武爾斯特享年34歲，埋葬在愛爾蘭庫迪 懷可福德懷可福德。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Prendergast
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Assumpta, Brendan, Colm, Declan.

Irish (of Welsh origin): habitational name from Prendergast (Pembrokeshire). The placename might contain Welsh prender(w) ‘oak tree’ + gast ‘bitch’, hence ‘oak tree frequented by dogs’, but this is far from certain.

History: Maurice Prendergast was one of the leaders of a small force of Norman knights and archers who landed at Bannow in Wexford in 1169, the advance guard of the invasion in 1170 by Richard de Clare, Earl of Pembroke (‘Strongbow’). Maurice was granted lands in Wexford, and was the progenitor of the Irish families that migrated to North America. According to family tradition the surname derives from a warrior called Prenliregast, who supposedly fought at Hastings (the name Preudirlegast is listed in a 16th-century version of the Battle Abbey Roll), and who took his name from a ‘lost’ village in Flanders called Brontegeest. Flemings did indeed settle in Pembrokeshire in the 12th century, but the Battle Abbey Roll is an unreliable document, created long after the Conquest, and no evidence has been found for this or any other surname in medieval Britain, Ireland, the Netherlands, or Belgium being derived from a place called Brontegeest.

variants.name: Pendergast
meanings: Irish and Welsh: variant of   Prendergast  .

variants.name: Pendergrast
meanings: Irish and Welsh: variant of   Prendergast  . This form of the surname is rare in Britain and Ireland.

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

nameMeanings.name: or
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally the name of the god of thunder in Norse mythology, Thor (Old Norse Þórr). It was not used as a personal name during the Middle Ages. The modern name is either a late 18th-century revival of the god's name or else a vernacular development of Tord .

variants.name: Thor
meanings: Originally the name of the god of thunder in Norse mythology, Thor (Old Norse Þórr). It was not used as a personal name during the Middle Ages. The modern name is either a late 18th-century revival of the god's name or else a vernacular development of Tord .

nameMeanings.name: Matilda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

variants.name: Mathilda
meanings: Latinized form of a Germanic personal name derived from maht, meht ‘might’ + hild ‘battle’. This was the name of an early German queen ( 895–968 ), wife of Henry the Fowler, who was noted for her piety and generosity. It was also the name of the wife of William the Conqueror and of the daughter of Henry I of England ( see Maud ). The name was introduced into England by the Normans, and this Latinized form is the one that normally occurs in medieval records, while the vernacular form Maud was the one in everyday use. Matilda was revived in England as a learned form in the 18th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud or Matilda de Prendergast was born on 17 March 1242, in Enniscorthy, County Wexford, Ireland, her father, Sir Gerald de Prendergast Lord of Enniscorthy, was 56 and her mother, Matilda de Burgh, was 14. She married Maurice FitzMaurice 3rd Lord of Offaly about 1258. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. She died on 26 July 1276, in Ulster, Northern Ireland, United Kingdom, at the age of 34, and was buried in Wexford, County Wexford, Ireland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud or Matilda de Prendergast est née le 17 mars 1242, à Enniscorthy, County Wexford, Irlande, son père, Sir Gerald de Prendergast Lord of Enniscorthy, avait 56 ans et sa mère, Matilda de Burgh, avait 14 ans. Elle a épousé Maurice FitzMaurice 3rd Lord of Offaly environ 1258. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 26 juillet 1276, à Ulster, Irlande du Nord, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 34 ans, et a été enterrée à Wexford, County Wexford, Irlande.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maud or Matilda de Prendergast am 17. März 1242 in Enniscorthy, County Wexford, Irland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Gerald de Prendergast Lord of Enniscorthy 56 und ihre Mutter Matilda de Burgh 14. Sie hat um 1258 Maurice FitzMaurice 3rd Lord of Offaly geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 26. Juli 1276 im Alter von 34 Jahren in Ulster, Nordirland, United Kingdom und wurde in Wexford, County Wexford, Irland begraben.

Lei sposato Maurice FitzMaurice 3rd Lord of Offaly circa 1258. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì il 26 luglio 1276, nel Ulster, Irlanda del Nord, Regno Unito, all'età di 34 anni, e fu sepolta a Wexford, County Wexford, Irlanda.

ja.lifeSummary: Maud or Matilda de Prendergastが1242年3月17日にアイルランドコウントイ ウエクスフォードエニスコルシーで生まれた時，父Sir Gerald de Prendergast Lord of Enniscorthyは56歳，母Matilda de Burghは14歳であった。約1258年にMaurice FitzMaurice 3rd Lord of Offalyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1276年7月26日にイギリス北アイルランドウルスターで34歳で死去し，アイルランドコウントイ ウエクスフォードウエクスフォードにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Maud or Matilda de Prendergast(이)가 1242년 3월 17일 아일랜드 카운티 w스퍼드 니스코르시에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Gerald de Prendergast Lord of Enniscorthy(은)는 56세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Matilda de Burgh(은)는 14세였다. 그녀는 대략 1258년 Maurice FitzMaurice 3rd Lord of Offaly(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1276년 7월 26일 영국 북아일랜드 울스터에서 34세의 나이로 사망했으며 아일랜드 카운티 w스퍼드 w스퍼드에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Maurice FitzMaurice 3rd Lord of Offaly aproximadamente 1258. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 26 de julho de 1276, em Ulster, Irlanda do Norte, United Kingdom, com 34 anos, e foi sepultada em Wexford, County Wexford, Irlanda.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud or Matilda de Prendergast родилась 17 марта 1242 г. в местности эннискорти, коунти вексфорд, Ирландия, её отцу по имени Sir Gerald de Prendergast Lord of Enniscorthy было 56 лет, а матери, которую звали Matilda de Burgh, 14. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Maurice FitzMaurice 3rd Lord of Offaly около 1258 г. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Она умерла 26 июля 1276 г. в местности улстир, Северная Ирландия, Великобритания в возрасте 34 лет и была похоронена в местности вексфорд, коунти вексфорд, Ирландия.
